calendar.matters:
Petition For Order: 1. Allowing Statutory Administrators And Attorneys Compensation; And 2. For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Marcia Rosser (Petitioner).
Brian Connors for John Mangini (Administrator of the Estate of Raymond Thrush).
Byron Done for The Maria Montessori School of The Golden Gate.

The Court noted that there is a notice issue as to Mr. Mangini. Mr. Connors stated that notice is being waived. Ms. Calhoun stated that the issue of payment to Mr. Payette has been resolved, that the current assets on hand as of July 31, 2019 are $75,908.57 and that she will file a copy of the bank statement today. Mr. Done stated that the school does have standing/has a property interest, that the Personal Representative entered into a lease agreement on the property that belongs to the estate and rent has been collected in the estate, that he is seeking to have the lease transferred to the trust pursuant to Probate Code 9651 and that it is the Personal Representative's duty to transfer it over to the proper entity. The Court stated that this issue is not before it/not in a petition. Ms. Calhoun stated that she was not aware the property was in the trust until she got involved in October 2018, that the agreement was drafted by Mr. Done, that her Client had been very ill and signed the lease agreement by mistake, that Mr. Done doesn't have standing to ask for an accounting, that title is held in the trust and that she is asking to have this estate closed. In addition, Ms. Calhoun stated that she has encouraged Mr. Connors to negotiate with Mr. Done. Mr. Connors stated that he agrees with Ms. Calhoun that this is a matter that has to involve the trust and that there is no reason why the estate can't be closed. Mr. Done indicated that he was involved with drafting of the lease agreement. The Petition for Order Allowing Statutory Administrator's and Attorneys' Compensation and for Final Distribution is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order: 1. Allowing Statutory Administrators And Attorneys Compensation; And 2. For Final Distribution

Petition For Order: 1. Allowing Statutory Administrators And Attorneys Compensation; And 2. For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Continued To May-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Marcia Rosser.
Byron Done for Maria Montessori School of The Golden Gate.

Ms. Calhoun stated that Raymond's Father (Roland) predeceased him, made reference to Mr. Payette's declaration regarding his search for heirs, spoke of efforts made by her Paralegal, stated that the trust property is in the trust, that she noticed the Public Administrator, that Raymond was the Trustee and that there weren't any remainder beneficiaries named. The Court stated that what has been done does not seem like a thorough search, that it is not satisfied with what has been done and orders that a further search for Roland's heirs and the Mother's heirs be done. Ms. Calhoun stated that they have a potential resolution, that it involves the trust and her Client acting as the Trustee of the trust, that they will then do a search through the trust, negotiate a lease that is more favorable to the ultimate heirs and appoint a successor trustee, and that notice will go out when the trust proceeding is filed. Ms. Calhoun also stated that there is no lease for the real property, that it was signed by the Trustee, that a petition to approve a settlement will be heard in a trust proceeding, that there is about $66,000.00 (about $33,000.00 in cash) in assets in the estate, and that they have requested to close out the estate. In addition, Ms. Calhoun stated that Mr. Payette's clients have no standing, that the Public Administrators are the owners of the property until heirs are found and come in for Raymond's estate, that she has a copy of a check to Mr. Payette of November 29, 2004 in the amount of $13,304.31, asked for permission to use estate funds to pay for filing fees of the trust and legal fees, and stated that she has asked Mr. Done to dismiss this proceeding. Mr. Done stated that he agrees to have this petition and the trust petition heard together, and that he wants to preserves his Client's rights. The Court stated that the proposal makes sense, but there is still this issue of notice. The Petition for Order Allowing Fees and for Final Distribution is continued to May 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for completion of the heir search. The Court will sign an order for payment of the heir search for up to $5,000.00. The Court also stated that it would like the parties to have a signed settlement agreement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Petition For Order: 1. Allowing Statutory Administrators And Attorneys Compensation; And 2. For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Continued To Jan-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emilie M. Calhoun for and with Marcia Rosser (Executor);
Byron Done appearing by CourtCall for Maria Montessori School of the Golden Gate (Objector).

The Court stated that it had concerns with regard to the heir search. Ms. Calhoun addressed the declaration filed by Mr. Payette. The Court also stated that a final Inventory & Appraisal was not filed. Ms. Calhoun stated that Mr. Payette needs to congnitively respond, that Mr. Done filed an objection, that there was a misimpression all these years that the school was in the Estate, and that school property has increased in value. Ms. Calhoun also stated that Mr. Payette had her client sign the new lease, that Mr. Done wants her client to sign as Trustee, and that they did not know until last year that the school is in the Trust. Mr. Done addressed the lease being signed as Executor and not as Trustee. Ms. Calhoun stated that they attempted to get a real estate person to come in and that Mr. Done should open a Trust proceeding to see who owns this property. In addition, Ms. Calhoun stated that her client didn't have adequate information, addressed a possible surcharge if they find that the property is under valued, that her client is a victim in all of this, that her client needs access to the property to have it analyzed, and that Mr. Done's client doesn't have standing to object to the current petition. Additonal argument by Mr. Done with regard to the lease is held. The Court stated it would continue the matter for an heir search and for the filing of a final Inventory & Appraisal. The Court stated that counsel should get something on file noting that the options down the road would possibly be that Ms. Rosser would either be asked to sign as Trustee or a move to set aside the lease. Ms. Calhoun noted that the litigation is costing the Estate. The Court then continued the matter to January 15, 2019 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 and noted that if something is filed by counsel that the lease issue would be continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)
